agent.name: Support Triage Agent
agent.description: Classifies inbound tickets, chooses the next action, and drafts concise customer replies.

agent.name: Refund Review Agent
agent.description: Reviews refund requests, checks policy constraints, and explains approval decisions.

run.prompt: Classify the customer request, decide the next action, and draft a reply.
run.response: The customer is blocked by account verification. The agent should request a refreshed identity check and provide a direct support handoff.
run.rationale: The message contains account access symptoms and a failed verification attempt, so the lowest-friction resolution is a guided identity check instead of a billing escalation.
run.decision: Escalate to identity verification with a prepared customer reply.

conversation.content: I cannot access my account after the verification screen keeps looping.
I found a verification loop. I can help you restart the identity check and route this to the account safety team.

tool_call.name: lookupAccountStatus
tool_call.summary: Account is active but verification is stale.

run.prompt: Classify the customer request and determine whether a refund, bug, or account workflow applies.
run.response: The agent classified the request as billing, but the conversation also includes a product bug signal that should be sent to engineering.
run.rationale: The user asks for a refund after a failed export. Billing intent is present, but the failed export should be captured as a product defect before a final refund decision.
run.decision: Send to human review because billing and product-defect signals conflict.

conversation.content: The export failed three times and I want my monthly charge refunded.
I can document the failed export and route the refund request for review.

tool_call.name: searchIncidentLog
tool_call.summary: Found multiple export failures for the same tenant.

run.prompt: Evaluate whether the customer refund request satisfies policy and explain the decision.
run.response: The refund should be approved because the customer experienced repeated export failures within the current billing window.
run.rationale: The policy allows refunds when a core workflow fails repeatedly and support logs confirm the incident.
run.decision: Approve refund and attach incident evidence to the case.

conversation.content: Please review this refund request for repeated export failures.
The request meets policy because the failed export is confirmed and unresolved.

tool_call.name: lookupRefundPolicy
tool_call.summary: Policy allows refund for confirmed workflow outage.

tool_call.name: attachIncidentEvidence
tool_call.summary: Attached incident IDs to refund case.
